title: My Leni as mom, grand mom, and professor
sub_topics: Asian Institute of Management (AIM) — 37 years professor
Bukas Loob Sa Diyos Catholic Charismatic Community (BLD)

stances.claim: Leni was 'always so soft-spoken, big-hearted, selfless, and so generous,' raising the five children 'with quiet authority' — patient, kind, wise — never yelling, reasoning rather than sermonizing (she said 'eight was the age of reason').
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission via daughter's eulogy + structured-virtue enumeration

stances.claim: Leni's monthly-allowance system taught the children how banking, saving, and parental safety-nets work — those who ran out before month-end starved (and could borrow with installment repayment); those who saved still got things they needed bought for them anyway.
stances.rhetorical_move: concrete parenting-philosophy anecdote transmitted via Celine's eulogy

stances.claim: In the BLD Catholic Charismatic Community, CJ and Leni served as members of the Council of Servant Leaders — CJ introduced a joyful, animated worship format, while Leni remained his quiet anchor.

Leni at AIM was 'frail and delicate' yet one of four women in the male-dominated pioneering 1971 batch, balancing 'the demands of the Harvard case-study method' with being mother to five children and a good wife — inspiring her classmate Mari to 'chase after my dream no matter what.'
stances.rhetorical_move: transmission via classmate's eulogy + role-modeling framing

institutions: Bukas Loob Sa Diyos Catholic Charismatic Community
events: BLD Council of Servant Leaders service

signature_phrases: eight was the age of reason
register_markers: Last Monday, I published my eulogy
let me quote portions (within my limited space)
Tagalog/Filipino familial vocatives (Tita, Lola, Ate, Mommy Not)
decision_framework_signals: frames spousal partnership as complementary-temperaments (animated CJ + quiet Leni anchor)
uses concrete-parenting-anecdote (monthly allowance) as teaching-philosophy evidence
applies role-modeling lens for working women balancing career and family
cross_references: Last Monday, I published my eulogy during the wake of my Leni
notable_anecdotes: Leni giving the five children monthly (not weekly) allowances, creating banking-and-saving lessons — kids who ran out starved but could borrow against next month; kids who saved got needed items bought anyway
Granddaughter-grandson Rafa staying in Dasma house, being greeted with chicheria offers and 'Do you have a girlfriend na?'
Leni teaching Rafa to curl his tongue, play Uno, Old Maid, Sudoku — but never figuring out how to raise both eyebrows
Leni's love of nice phones, with Rafa helping her set them up and playing Bounce together — 'one of the big reasons why I love phones and tech'
Leni in the pioneering 1971 AIM batch — one of four women, looking frail and delicate, managing Harvard case-study method while mothering five children
Leni and CJ as BLD Council of Servant Leaders introducing joyful animated worship — Leni 'remained his anchor'
